title: Recover Rust RPL hardening
description: Recover Rust RPL hardening with RFC-correct foreign-instance/DODAG filtering, lollipop comparison, admissible parent cleanup/failover, optional DAO DODAGID parsing, and route-installation scope checks. Rewrite incorrect checkpoint D=0 and raw-rank rejection hunks.
acceptance_criteria: Global D=0 DAO parses with options at byte 4; local D=0 rejects; D=1 requires 20 bytes and round-trips; root rejects foreign instance or mismatched present DODAGID before route mutation; foreign DIOs and older/incomparable versions are ignored; saturated and MaxRank-inadmissible parents are removed while valid downward failover remains possible; RFC boundary vectors, lichen-rpl/lichen-node tests, all-feature clippy, and three reviews pass.
notes: All out-of-directory Rust RPL recovery work is committed. integration/rust-rpl-recovery ends at 38f579532; integration/rust-prefix-model ends at 29437e6e0. Worktrees were not merged because prefix/path-snapshot reconciliation remains unresolved. Continue on Linux from preserved branches.
